message:
test(e2e): tighten bd-db300.5.5.3 protocol artifact field assertions

Extends `assert_protocol_artifact_fields` in the
`bd_db300_5_5_3_writer_routing_protocol` e2e harness with a
second required-field loop that walks the
`artifact.comparison` sub-object and asserts every downstream
evidence consumer field is present.

The previous assertion only covered the top-level artifact
fields; the comparison block could silently lose a field (e.g.
if a new writer-routing metric was added to the synthetic
summary but not propagated to the serialized artifact) and the
test would still pass.

The new loop asserts the presence of 11 comparison fields that
the downstream evidence pipeline treats as load-bearing:

- `trace_id` — per-run correlation
- `scenario_id` — which scenario produced this comparison
- `placement_profile` — which placement profile was used
- `workload_shape` — disjoint / overlapping / hot-page
- `conflict_rate_reduction` — routed vs baseline conflict
  delta
- `retry_rate_reduction` — retry delta
- `fallback_rate_reduction` — fallback decision delta
- `publication_retry_rate_reduction` — publication retry
  delta
- `remote_ownership_events_reduction` — remote ownership
  events delta
- `ops_per_sec_improvement` — throughput improvement
- `fairness_jain_delta` — Jain fairness index delta between
  baseline and routed

Each field's assertion message names the missing field
directly so a regression failure immediately identifies what
was dropped, rather than forcing the developer to diff the
serialized JSON.
